title: Choking — Adult
immediate_action: Ask: 'Are you choking?' If they cannot speak, cough, or breathe — act immediately.

steps.instruction: Encourage them to cough forcefully if they can.
steps.tip: If they can cough or speak, their airway is partially open. Stay with them and encourage coughing.

steps.instruction: Give 5 back blows. Stand behind, lean them forward. Strike firmly between the shoulder blades with the heel of your hand.
steps.tip: Use enough force. Each blow should be a separate attempt to dislodge the object.

steps.instruction: Give 5 abdominal thrusts (Heimlich maneuver). Stand behind, wrap arms around waist. Make a fist above the navel, below the ribcage. Pull sharply inward and upward.
steps.tip: Quick upward thrust — like you're trying to lift them off the ground.

steps.instruction: Alternate: 5 back blows → 5 abdominal thrusts. Repeat until the object is expelled or the person becomes unconscious.
steps.tip: Check the mouth between cycles. Remove visible objects — never do a blind finger sweep.

steps.instruction: If the person becomes unconscious, lower them to the ground. Call emergency services. Begin CPR — 30 compressions, check mouth, 2 breaths.
steps.tip: Chest compressions may dislodge the object. Check the mouth before each rescue breath.

when_to_stop: Person becomes unconscious — switch to CPR
do_not: Do NOT do abdominal thrusts on pregnant women (use chest thrusts instead)
Do NOT do blind finger sweeps
Do NOT slap the back while the person is upright
